floor_history.headline: fable-blitz recon: packed attribute is LOAD-BEARING (it alone drives lwl/lwr/swl/swr selection) but the clean respell is a natural-alignment-2 struct of four s16 (PsyQ SVECTOR shape) - emit_block_move

frontier.hypothesis: UNMEASURED: replace PackedPair_233AC with a plain `typedef struct { s16 a, b, c, d; } SVec8_233AC;` (or the project's SVECTOR if already declared) and copy via that type - expect the identical lwl/lwr/swl/swr sequence and distance 0
frontier.mechanism: GCC 2.7.2 expand_block_move with align 2 < UNITS_PER_WORD emits movsi_ulw/movsi_usw word pairs in loads-then-stores order for a size-8 copy - the same RTL the packed (align 1) form produces; offsets 0x3B/0x38 etc. are alignment-independent
frontier.next_probe: sandbox func_800233AC --disable all with the s16-member struct substituted at src/code6cac.c:2895+2952

frontier.hypothesis: UNMEASURED contingency: if the s16-member form perturbs scheduling (block-move heuristics keyed on exact align 1 vs 2), fall back to member-shape variants
frontier.mechanism: mips.c block-move sequence selection can differ per alignment class; align 2 and align 1 both take the unaligned path for word-sized chunks, so risk is low
frontier.next_probe: sweep {u16 x4}, {s16 a,b; s16 c,d;} nested, and an align-1 u8[8] struct variant via tools/sweep_variants.py

frontier.hypothesis: UNMEASURED hygiene check: confirm PackedPair_233AC has no other users and out1's s16[4] declaration is untouched by the respell
frontier.mechanism: the lh loads at asm/funcs/func_800233AC.s:99-100 depend on out1 staying s16-typed at sp+0x38
frontier.next_probe: grep PackedPair_233AC across src/ (expected: the typedef + single use) before editing

judge_constraints: REGRESSION DIAGNOSIS (completion bar: match must land WITHOUT this construct family): CHEAT: `__attribute__((packed))` on PackedPair_233AC (src/code6cac.c:2896) is an alignment-coercion lever that forces GCC to emit lwl/lwr/swl/swr instead of lw/sw for the 8-byte struct copy at line 2953. The struct has only s32 members (no padding to eliminate), so packed has zero semantic effect; its only purpose is instruction selection. The original match commit (77f2494e) named this explicitly: 'packed struct lwl/lwr'. A worker must find a pure-C form that generates the unaligned load/store pairs without the packed coercion — the legitimate path is direct s32-from-u8* casts (arg0 is u8*, so arg0+0x98 carries 1-byte alignment naturally → swl/swr; out1 is s16[], alignment 2 → lwl/lwr from s32-pointer cast) rather than a purpose-built packed struct. Remove PackedPair_233AC and its use; replace with direct cast copies that let GCC derive the unalignment from the actual C types.  (committed code flagged by the re-audit patrol; review and re-do in pure C if confirmed. The byte-correct construct stays on main until a clean replacement lands.)
